data.title: Ansell Director Increases Indirect Shareholding via Voluntary Share Purchase Plan
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ansell ( (AU:ANN) ) has provided an update.

Ansell has disclosed a change in the indirect shareholding of director Randy Stone, reflecting his participation in the company’s Voluntary Share Purchase Plan. Through an account held by CPU Share Plans Pty Ltd as trustee for the ANN VSP control account, Stone acquired 382 ordinary shares on 18 March 2026, increasing his indirect holding from 365 to 747 shares.

The transaction, valued at approximately $11,400, was not conducted during a closed trading period and required no prior written clearance from the company. While modest in size, the purchase signals continued engagement by board members with Ansell’s equity, which can be viewed as a sign of alignment between director and shareholder interests.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:ANN) stock is a Hold with a A$36.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ansell stock, see the AU:ANN Stock Forecast page.

More about Ansell

Ansell Limited is a global manufacturer and marketer of protective solutions, best known for its industrial and medical gloves and personal protective equipment. The company focuses on safety products for healthcare, industrial and specialty markets, supplying customers worldwide with protective gear designed to reduce workplace and clinical risks.

Average Trading Volume: 353,036

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: A$4.23B

MFF Capital Investments Ltd. ( (AU:MFF) ) has shared an update.

MFF Capital Investments Ltd. has reported a change in director Chris Mackay’s holdings, reflecting his direct and indirect interests in the company and related funds. Through various entities and associated parties, Mackay maintains substantial exposure to MFF’s ordinary shares as well as units in the Montaka Global Fund – Active ETF and the Montaka Global Extension Fund – Complex ETF.

The latest notice shows Mackay acquired 64,037 additional fully paid ordinary MFF shares via on-market purchases at prices between $4.495 and $4.53 per share. Following these transactions, his total holding in MFF increased to 126,748,539 shares, underscoring continued director alignment with shareholders and confidence in the company’s investment strategy.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:MFF) stock is a Buy with a A$6.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on MFF Capital Investments Ltd. stock, see the AU:MFF Stock Forecast page.

More about MFF Capital Investments Ltd.

MFF Capital Investments Limited is a listed investment company focused on managing a portfolio of global equities. The firm invests in international companies and also fully owns Montaka Global Pty Ltd, the investment manager of the Montaka Global Fund – Active ETF and the Montaka Global Extension Fund – Complex ETF, giving it an integrated funds management and investment structure.

data.title: Woolworths Issues New Unquoted Equity Rights Under Employee Incentive Schemes
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Woolworths Group Ltd ( (AU:WOW) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Woolworths Group Limited has notified the market of the issue of unquoted equity securities under its employee incentive schemes, comprising 188,119 performance rights (WOWAK) and 209,628 share rights (WOWAA) issued on 16 March 2026. These securities are subject to transfer restrictions and will not be quoted on the ASX until those restrictions end, reflecting the company’s ongoing use of equity-based incentives to reward and retain employees and align their interests with shareholders.

The new unquoted rights issuance underscores Woolworths Group’s continued reliance on long-term incentive structures as part of its remuneration strategy. By expanding its pool of performance and share rights, the retailer is reinforcing a focus on performance-linked compensation, which can influence executive behaviour, cost of capital, and future dilution for existing shareholders once any vesting and restriction periods lapse.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:WOW) stock is a Buy with a A$35.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Woolworths Group Ltd stock, see the AU:WOW Stock Forecast page.

More about Woolworths Group Ltd

Woolworths Group Limited is a major Australian retail group listed on the ASX under the code WOW. The company operates primarily in food and everyday needs retailing and is a key player in the Australian consumer and supermarket sector.

Average Trading Volume: 2,745,640

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: Harvest Technology Group Sets Fully Virtual General Meeting for April 2026
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Harvest Technology Group Ltd. ( (AU:HTG) ).

Harvest Technology Group has called a virtual general meeting of shareholders for 1:00pm AWST on 20 April 2026, providing electronic access to the notice of meeting and explanatory materials through its website and ASX announcements. The company is moving away from routine dispatch of hard-copy documents, instead relying on digital distribution for investors who have not specifically elected to receive printed materials.

Shareholders will be able to view the meeting live, ask questions orally or in writing, and vote in real time online using their SRN or HIN credentials. The board is strongly encouraging investors to lodge proxy forms ahead of the meeting, preferably appointing the chair as proxy, with clear instructions and cut-off times provided for online, mail, email and mobile proxy submissions.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:HTG) stock is a Sell with a A$0.01 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Harvest Technology Group Ltd. stock, see the AU:HTG Stock Forecast page.

More about Harvest Technology Group Ltd.

Harvest Technology Group Ltd is an Australian technology company based in Bentley, Western Australia. It focuses on providing technology-driven solutions and services, with its shares listed on the ASX under the code HTG and a shareholder base that engages via digital investor communications.

data.title: Qualitas Real Estate Income Fund Maintains 7% Yield With Stable Portfolio in February 2026
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Qualitas Real Estate Income Fund Units ( (AU:QRI) ) just unveiled an update.

Qualitas Real Estate Income Fund reported a 7.00% per annum distribution on net asset value for February 2026, equivalent to $0.008592 per unit, with a unit price of $1.57 versus a unit NAV of $1.6004. The portfolio totals 58 loans with a weighted loan maturity of 1.12 years, 95% in senior loans and 5% in mezzanine loans, and a weighted loan‑to‑value ratio of 65%.

The manager confirmed there were no work‑outs, interest arrears or impairments as at 28 February 2026, underscoring stable credit performance across the portfolio. Net returns since inception stand at 7.44%, with a 1‑year net return of 7.12% and a spread of 3.35 percentage points above the RBA cash rate over one year, highlighting the fund’s ability to deliver income ahead of cash while maintaining diversification by borrower, sector and geography.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:QRI) stock is a Buy with a A$1.60 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Qualitas Real Estate Income Fund Units stock, see the AU:QRI Stock Forecast page.

More about Qualitas Real Estate Income Fund Units

Qualitas Real Estate Income Fund (ASX:QRI) is an Australian listed investment trust focused on real estate private credit. The fund provides predominantly senior and mezzanine loans secured against residential, commercial, industrial and retail property, with a portfolio concentrated in New South Wales, Victoria and Queensland. It targets returns linked to the RBA cash rate plus a margin, offering regular monthly distributions to unitholders.

data.title: Diversified Royalty Lifts 2025 Cash Flow as New AIR MILES Deal Bolsters Stability
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Diversified Royalty Corp ( (TSE:DIV) ).

Diversified Royalty Corp. reported 2025 revenue of $70.8 million and adjusted revenue of $76.1 million, reflecting high single-digit growth, while distributable cash rose 12.7% to $50.5 million and the annual payout ratio improved slightly to 88.1% despite higher dividends per share. Organic royalty growth moderated versus 2024 but remained positive at 4.1% for the year, supported by strong same-store sales growth at key partner Mr. Lube + Tires, solid fixed royalty contributions from partners such as Nurse Next Door, Stratus, BarBurrito and Cheba Hut, ongoing royalty relief to Sutton, and a newly amended AIR MILES agreement that replaces declining variable royalties with a long-term, escalating fixed royalty guaranteed by Bank of Montreal, enhancing cash flow visibility and stability for investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:DIV) stock is a Buy with a C$4.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Diversified Royalty Corp stock, see the TSE:DIV Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on DIV Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DIV is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by mixed financial performance: strong profitability and growth but meaningfully constrained by negative/volatile free cash flow and rising leverage. Technicals are supportive with price above major moving averages and positive MACD, while valuation is helped by a high dividend yield but tempered by a mid-range P/E.

To see Spark’s full report on DIV stock, click here.

More about Diversified Royalty Corp

Diversified Royalty Corp. is a Canadian royalty company that acquires royalties from a portfolio of established businesses across sectors such as automotive services, quick-service restaurants, professional services, and loyalty programs. Its revenue is derived from both variable and fixed royalty streams from partners including Mr. Lube + Tires, Oxford Learning, Mr. Mikes, Nurse Next Door, Stratus, BarBurrito, Cheba Hut, Sutton, and the AIR MILES loyalty program, providing diversified, cash-generating assets for shareholders.

data.title: Asset Plus Flags Munroe Lane Valuation Drop and NTA Impact
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Asset Plus Limited ( (NZ:APL) ).

Asset Plus Limited has received a final independent valuation of $105.5 million for its Munroe Lane property as at 31 March 2026, down from $107.0 million a year earlier. The decline is mainly attributed to a 0.25% softening in the capitalisation rate, reflecting recent comparable market transactions in the commercial property sector.

Since 30 September 2025, the company has invested in capital expenditure for the Aderant tenancy and speculative fit-outs of vacant space at Munroe Lane. Combined with other capitalised leasing costs and the lower valuation, this is expected to drive a $7.1 million reduction in the property’s fair value for the half-year, implying a forecast 1.9 cent decrease in net tangible assets to 30.5 cents per share, pending external audit review.

More about Asset Plus Limited

Asset Plus Limited, listed on the NZX as APL, is a New Zealand property investment company. Its portfolio includes assets such as the Munroe Lane property, where it undertakes tenant-specific fit-outs and speculative leasing works to enhance income and asset value over time.

Average Trading Volume: 68,608

Current Market Cap: N$68.92M

Adairs Ltd. ( (AU:ADH) ) has shared an announcement.

Adairs Limited has updated its previously announced dividend notification relating to the six‑month period ended 28 December 2025 for its ordinary fully paid shares. The record date for the dividend remains 11 March 2026, with an ex‑dividend date of 10 March 2026 and payment scheduled in due course.

The latest update, dated 20 March 2026, specifically adds the Dividend Reinvestment Plan pricing details that were absent from the 23 February 2026 release. This clarification provides shareholders with final terms for participating in the DRP, supporting informed capital allocation decisions and offering an alternative to cash dividends for investors seeking to reinvest.

data.title: NU E Power Clarifies Status of Global Hybrid Power Projects as Early-Stage and Non-Binding
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

NU E Power Corp. ( (TSE:NUE) ) just unveiled an update.

NU E Power Corp. has issued a clarifying statement on previously announced hybrid power development opportunities in Brazil, Mongolia, Canada, Malaysia and Nigeria, emphasizing that it and ACT Mid Market Ltd. do not directly own those assets and that no physical assets were acquired. The company now specifies that it holds exclusive options and development rights only for early-stage projects in Darkhan, Mongolia, and Saskatchewan, Canada, while other initiatives in Malaysia, Brazil and Nigeria remain non-binding, exploratory or at memorandum-of-understanding stages with no binding construction, offtake, or financing agreements in place.

For the Mongolian Darkhan Energy Park, NU E has paid for a 12‑month exclusive right to use the property and plans a joint development structure with a 60 percent interest pending feasibility and permits, while its Saskatchewan community power and data center hub is being jointly developed with XBASE on a similar 12‑month site-rights basis. The company also clarifies that campus data center discussions with Malaysian universities are limited to letters of intent, that the Minas Gerais solar project is subject to further due diligence and remains non-binding, that the Lagos hybrid project is at a very preliminary stage, and that its current CEO was not in that role or on the board at the time of the original 2025 announcement, underscoring the early and contingent nature of these ventures for investors and partners.

More about NU E Power Corp.

NU E Power Corp. is a Calgary-based multi-stage power developer focused on converting land and grid access into institutional-grade energy assets for the digital and global power economies. The company develops next-generation hybrid power sites that combine renewables, grid power, gas, nuclear and battery storage to deliver scalable, reliable and optimized energy infrastructure worldwide.

Average Trading Volume: 71,717

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: C$9.82M

See more data about NUE stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.
